title: Boston’s innovation economy hits a new milestone in global rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Five Boston-area firms made the LexisNexis 'Top 100 Global Innovators' list, a record for the region.
Orna Therapeutics was acquired last month by Eli Lilly for $2.4 billion, joining the list alongside emerging Boston-area firms.
Boston ties with L.A. for third in U.S. total companies on the LexisNexis list, behind Silicon Valley and New York City.

title: Is Scituate native Conor Garland a worthwhile trade target for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Conor Garland is signed with the Canucks through 2031-32, carrying a $6 million annual cap hit.
The Bruins are considering a trade for Garland, needing middle-six scoring to secure playoff positioning.
Garland's local ties and play style could appeal, but his fit with the Bruins' rebuilding timeline is uncertain.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington saw a sale of a one-family Colonial built in 1901 with 3 bedrooms for $363,581.
A Boston high-rise condo at 100 Belvidere St. with 2 bedrooms and 3 baths sold for $3,700,000.
Belmont had a condo/apt sale at 20 Chester Road with 4 bedrooms and 2 baths for $1,279,000.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alec Pierce may enter free agency as Colts choose transition tag for QB Daniel Jones.
Pierce, 25, led NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025, logging 1,003 yards last season.
Patriots could pursue Pierce, but may need to restructure Stefon Diggs' contract to afford him.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Vermont postal worker, Natasha Morisseau, 34, was cited for allegedly discarding mail intended for delivery.
Police found discarded mail in a North Troy dumpster, including packages and holiday cards, some containing money.
Morisseau faces nine petty larceny counts and was suspended by USPS; court appearance set for March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man in his 80s and a dog died in a house fire on Granite Street in Foxborough early Tuesday.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville firefighters extinguished the fire within an hour, but the house sustained major damage.
The fire's cause is under investigation; Foxborough Fire Chief Michael Kelleher expressed condolences to the victim's family.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's restaurant faces a potential license violation for hosting a party with Patriots players involving strippers and after-hours drinking.
Owner Helder Brandao takes responsibility but blames players for bringing hookahs and their own alcohol, violating liquor license rules.
Boston Licensing Board will consider penalties, including possible suspension or revocation of Estella’s liquor license after hearing testimony.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest nationwide scores on AP exams, breaking records with 35.8% of graduates scoring 3 or higher.
Governor Maura Healey announced Massachusetts ranked third in graduates taking AP exams and first for Black students participating, excluding D.C.
The state subsidized over 90,783 AP exams for nearly 50,000 low-income students, supporting expanded access to college-level coursework.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy city councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Councilors receive a 50% raise from $29,129 to $44,500, initially approved by the previous council.
Many new councilors plan to donate their raises to nonprofits, as state laws prevent immediate repeal of pay increases.
Amid voter discontent, councilors pursue measures to review and potentially amend compensation processes for elected officials.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox remain a suitable trade partner for Isaac Paredes, despite their recent acquisition of Caleb Durbin.
Jarren Duran could be a trade chip for Boston, potentially creating more DH opportunities for Masataka Yoshida.
Red Sox have not recently contacted the Astros about Paredes, but adding him could boost their lineup power.
